title: OptimizerPlan
description: The LLM planner's structured output, parsed via .with_structured_output(OptimizerPlan) - the refined plan itself, proposed and not authoritative (every plan is gated by the constraint engine + confidence gate before anything is applied). Layer 1 of the plan contract; wrapped by PlanRecord for a cycle. See docs/specs/design/optimizer/05-spec-optimizer-plan-contract.md §2, governed by RFC-004.

TrajectoryPoint.description: One hour of the refined setpoint trajectory across the horizon.

at.description: The instant these targets apply (RFC 3339, UTC). trajectory[0].at is the cycle start; points are hour-spaced across the horizon.

ObjectiveScores.description: The planner's self-reported weighting of how each optimization objective shaped the plan. Advisory / explainability only - never consumed by the application gate.

EscalationHint.description: An optional planner self-flag (e.g. a target held near a bound). Advisory only - the authoritative escalation reason_code is assigned downstream by the gate, not taken from the model, so reason_code here is a free-form hint rather than the canonical enum.

reason_code.description: Advisory hint at why the planner is uncertain; not the authoritative canonical code.

trajectory.description: The refined setpoint trajectory across the horizon, one point per hour. A planning artifact held in memory so a skipped cycle can extend the plan - surfaced for review, never re-applied. On a skipped or held cycle nothing new is written: the last applied immediate_setpoints bundle stays in force (Phase 2 already holds it), so the trajectory is not written to Phase 2 - only a fresh, gated plan's immediate_setpoints reaches the greenhouse. Not the state-change gate's comparison input: the gate diffs the twin's predicted-climate forecast, a separate in-memory reference.

immediate_setpoints.description: The single next bundle the applier submits to Phase 2 this cadence (the refined targets of trajectory[0] as a partial patch). Must equal trajectory[0].setpoints field-for-field; JSON Schema cannot express this cross-field relationship, so it is enforced by the optimizer's constraint engine, not this schema. The one part of the plan that reaches the greenhouse.

confidence.description: The planner's self-assessed confidence in the immediate bundle. Load-bearing: below the configured confidence_threshold (default 0.8) the plan is escalated, not applied.

explanation.description: Short natural-language reason summary - the reasoning/audit trace surfaced to an operator on escalation or inspection.
